description: 32F walked into an open cabinet, no LOC, no amnesia, GCS 15 — Canadian CT Head must NOT fire
transcript:
Dr. Reyes: Hi, what's going on?
Patient: I was reaching into the upper cabinet at home, and I stood up into the corner of another cabinet door that was open. Hit the top of my head pretty hard.
Dr. Reyes: Did you pass out at all? Even briefly?
Patient: No, I just said a lot of bad words. Then I went and put ice on it.
Dr. Reyes: Any memory loss before or after?
Patient: No, I remember the whole thing. I was annoyed, that's why I came in — my husband insisted.
Dr. Reyes: Any vomiting, severe headache, vision changes?
Patient: No. A little tender where I hit, that's all.
Dr. Reyes: Any blood thinners?
Patient: No.
Dr. Reyes: How old are you?
Patient: Thirty-two. I'm healthy, no medications.
Nurse: GCS 15, alert and oriented x3. HR 76, BP 118/72, sat 99.
Dr. Reyes: Small tender area at the top of the head, no laceration, no obvious depression. Pupils equal, no focal deficits. No basal skull signs.
Dr. Reyes: This doesn't meet criteria for imaging. No LOC, no amnesia, low-energy mechanism, normal exam. We can observe briefly and discharge with head injury precautions.

groundTruth.notes: Canadian CT Head requires LOC, amnesia, or witnessed disorientation as the entry criterion. Without any of those, the rule does not apply. Surfacing it here would be noise — the doctor has correctly identified that this isn't even a candidate.
